landing.subtitle: Connect your development tools, define verification rules, and get complete traceability for every release. Honest infrastructure for teams that operate with rigor.
landing.register_desc: Sign up with your personal and organisation details. No admin approval — immediate access.
landing.step1_desc: Name, email, and organisation details. Less than 2 minutes.
landing.step2_desc: You will receive an activation link in your email.
landing.step3_desc: Create your organisation directly from your profile and start verifying.
feedback.modal_subtitle: Help me improve SVAES — your opinion matters
feedback.disclosure: Your name, rating and comment may be published in the project's GitHub README. Your email is never published — it's only used for the internal notification.
feedback.comments_placeholder: What's good? What's wrong? What would you improve?
feedback.error: Could not send feedback. Please try again.

reset_password.error.expired_token: This reset link has expired. Please request a new one.

profiles.expected_value_hint: Value the 'version' attribute must have on documents to pass this rule. If left empty, the rule will never pass.
profiles.approved_states_hint: Comma-separated list of statuses considered approved. If left empty, APROBADO and VALIDADO are used by default.
profiles.master_artifact_id_hint: Internal SVAES ID of the artifact that declares the planned task list (e.g. the ClickUp planning item), not its ID in the external tool. Leave empty to auto-detect the single 'PLAN' artifact in the release; only set this if a release has more than one and you need to disambiguate.
profiles.master_field_hint: Metadata field on the master artifact holding the declared task references (array or comma-separated text). Defaults to 'planned_tasks' if left empty.

legal.privacy.intro: This policy describes how personal data of SVAES platform users is collected, used and protected, in accordance with the <abbr title="General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679">GDPR</abbr> and the <abbr title="Spanish Organic Law 3/2018 on Personal Data Protection and Digital Rights Guarantee">LOPDGDD</abbr>.
legal.privacy.s1.title: Data Controller
legal.privacy.s2.title: Data Processed and Purposes
legal.privacy.s2.intro: Within the SVAES service, we process the following personal data:
legal.privacy.s2.item1: Identification and access data: e-mail address, username and role within the organisation. Purpose: user account management and platform access control.
legal.privacy.s2.item2: Session data: authentication tokens (JWT) stored in the browser's local storage (localStorage). Purpose: maintaining an active session during platform use. Deleted on logout.
legal.privacy.s2.item3: Activity data: access logs and operations performed on the platform (audit logs). Purpose: security, traceability and regulatory compliance.
legal.privacy.s2.footer: No special category data is collected (health, racial origin, beliefs, etc.).
legal.privacy.s3.title: Legal Basis for Processing
legal.privacy.s3.item1: Performance of a contract (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR): necessary for the provision of the service contracted by the user's organisation.
legal.privacy.s3.item2: Legitimate interests (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR): activity logging to ensure the security and integrity of the platform.
legal.privacy.s3.item3: Compliance with a legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR): retention of audit records in accordance with applicable regulations.
legal.privacy.s4.title: Retention Period
legal.privacy.s4.p1: Identification data will be retained while the user account remains active. Once deactivated, it will be deleted or anonymised within a maximum period of 12 months, unless there is a legal obligation to retain it.
legal.privacy.s4.p2: Session tokens stored in localStorage are automatically deleted on logout or upon token expiry.
legal.privacy.s4.p3: Audit logs will be retained for 6 months in accordance with applicable legal requirements.
legal.privacy.s5.title: Recipients and Data Processors
legal.privacy.s5.p1: Data is not shared with third parties unless legally required. For service delivery, we may engage data processors (infrastructure and hosting providers, etc.) who act under the controller's instructions and have signed the data processing agreements required by the <abbr title="General Data Protection Regulation">GDPR</abbr>.
legal.privacy.s5.p2: Current infrastructure providers are: Oracle Cloud.
legal.privacy.s6.title: International Transfers
legal.privacy.s6.p1: No international transfers of data outside the European Economic Area are made.
legal.privacy.s7.title: Data Subject Rights
legal.privacy.s7.intro: In accordance with the <abbr title="General Data Protection Regulation">GDPR</abbr> and the <abbr title="Spanish Organic Law 3/2018 on Personal Data Protection">LOPDGDD</abbr>, the user may exercise the following rights:
legal.privacy.s7.item1: Access: to know what personal data is being processed.
legal.privacy.s7.item2: Rectification: to correct inaccurate or incomplete data.
legal.privacy.s7.item3: Erasure: to request deletion of data when it is no longer necessary for the purposes for which it was collected.
legal.privacy.s7.item4: Restriction of processing: to request restriction of processing in the cases provided for by law.
legal.privacy.s7.item5: Portability: to receive data in a structured, commonly used and machine-readable format.
legal.privacy.s7.item6: Objection: to object to the processing of data on grounds relating to the individual's particular situation.
legal.privacy.s7.contact: To exercise these rights, write to UO295454@uniovi.es, stating the right you wish to exercise and enclosing a copy of your identity document.
legal.privacy.s7.authority: You have the right to lodge a complaint with the Spanish Data Protection Authority (AEPD) — www.aepd.es — if you consider that the processing of your data does not comply with applicable regulations.
legal.privacy.s8.title: Local Storage
legal.privacy.s8.intro: This platform uses the browser's local storage mechanism (localStorage) solely to maintain the authenticated user's session. The items stored are:
legal.privacy.s8.item1: access_token: JWT token for API access. Duration: until token expiry or logout.
legal.privacy.s8.item2: refresh_token: session renewal token. Duration: until logout.
legal.privacy.s8.item3: user: basic profile information (email, name, role). Duration: until logout.
legal.privacy.s8.footer: These data are technically necessary for the service to function and are not used for tracking, advertising or behavioural analysis. No tracking or advertising cookies are installed.
legal.privacy.s9.title: Changes to this Policy
legal.privacy.s9.p1: The controller reserves the right to amend this privacy policy to adapt it to legislative or case-law developments. Changes will take effect from their publication on the platform. Periodic review of this page is recommended.
legal.privacy.footer: Document drafted in accordance with the <abbr title="General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679">GDPR</abbr> and the <abbr title="Spanish Organic Law 3/2018 on Personal Data Protection and Digital Rights Guarantee">LOPDGDD</abbr>. Competent supervisory authority: <abbr title="Agencia Española de Protección de Datos">AEPD</abbr>.
legal.notice.intro: This website corresponds to the Final Degree Project entitled "Design and Development of an Automatic Software Delivery Verification System" (SVAES), developed by Adrián Martínez as a student at the School of Computer Engineering, University of Oviedo. The platform is exclusively academic in nature and does not constitute a commercial service.
legal.notice.s1.title: Owner Information
legal.notice.s1.intro: In compliance with Article 10 of Law 34/2002 of 11 July on Information Society Services and Electronic Commerce (<abbr title="Spanish Information Society Services and Electronic Commerce Act">LSSI-CE</abbr>), this website is owned by:
legal.notice.s1.name: Legal name
legal.notice.s1.registration_value: Not applicable — academic project with no registered commercial activity
legal.notice.s2.title: Purpose and Acceptance
legal.notice.s2.p1: SVAES is an automatic software delivery verification platform aimed at engineering teams. Accessing and using this platform implies full and unconditional acceptance of these legal terms.
legal.notice.s2.p2: The owner reserves the right to modify these terms, as well as the website content, at any time and without prior notice.
legal.notice.s3.title: Access and Use of the Platform
legal.notice.s3.p1: Access to the platform requires prior authentication using corporate credentials. Use is restricted to persons authorised by the contracting organisation. Unauthorised access, unauthorised reproduction of content and any use contrary to applicable law are prohibited.
legal.notice.s3.p2: The user undertakes to use the platform correctly, in accordance with applicable law, good faith, public order and these legal terms. The owner shall not be liable for any damages arising from improper use.
legal.notice.s4.title: Intellectual and Industrial Property
legal.notice.s4.p1: All platform content (source code, design, text, logos, trademarks and images) is the property of the owner or of third parties who have authorised its use, and is protected by Spanish and international intellectual and industrial property law. Reproduction, distribution, public communication or transformation without the owner's written authorisation is expressly prohibited.
legal.notice.s5.title: Disclaimer
legal.notice.s5.p1: The owner does not guarantee continuous and uninterrupted service availability, nor shall be liable for damage arising from technical interruptions, connectivity failures or force majeure. The verification results generated by the platform are for informational purposes only and do not constitute technical or legal advice.
legal.notice.s6.title: Personal Data Protection
legal.notice.s6.p1: The processing of users' personal data is governed by Regulation (EU) 2016/679 of the European Parliament and of the Council (GDPR), Organic Law 3/2018 of 5 December on Personal Data Protection and Digital Rights Guarantee (LOPDGDD), and the Privacy Policy available at all times on this platform.
legal.notice.s7.title: Applicable Law and Jurisdiction
legal.notice.s7.p1: These legal terms are governed by Spanish law. For the resolution of any dispute arising from the use of this platform, the parties submit to the Courts and Tribunals of Oviedo, expressly waiving any other jurisdiction that may apply.
legal.notice.footer: Document drafted in accordance with the <abbr title="Spanish Information Society Services and Electronic Commerce Act">LSSI-CE</abbr>, the <abbr title="General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679">GDPR</abbr> and the <abbr title="Spanish Organic Law 3/2018 on Personal Data Protection and Digital Rights Guarantee">LOPDGDD</abbr>.
project_new.profile_default_hint: The system default profile with all 10 standard verification rules will be used.
rule_evidence.ok.RV-01: Artifacts were found registered in the release.
rule_evidence.ok.RV-02: All artifact IDs are unique and consistent.
rule_evidence.ok.RV-03: All task-type artifacts have a valid status.
rule_evidence.ok.RV-04: All estimation and effort fields contain valid numeric values.
rule_evidence.ok.RV-05: The required documents were found in the release.
rule_evidence.ok.RV-06: All documents have their version correctly specified.
rule_evidence.ok.RV-07: The release is correctly registered in the planning system.
rule_evidence.ok.RV-08: The release planning is consistent with the registered artifacts.
rule_evidence.ok.RV-09: All code references are correctly linked to the release.
rule_evidence.ok.RV-10: The test report was found with an approved status.
rule_evidence.ok.has_duplicated_code: No duplicated code detected above the configured threshold.
rule_evidence.ok.has_high_severity_vulne: No high severity vulnerabilities detected in the code.
rule_evidence.ok.has_critical_vulnerabil: No critical vulnerabilities detected in the code.
rule_evidence.ok.has_open_high_priority_: No open high priority issues found.
rule_evidence.ok.has_security_hotspots: No security hotspots detected in the code.
rule_evidence.ok.has_uncovered_code: Test coverage is within the acceptable threshold.
rule_evidence.ok.has_blocking_issues: No blocking issues detected for this release.
rule_evidence.ok.meets_minimum_test_cove: Test coverage exceeds the minimum required threshold.
rule_evidence.ok.meets_maximum_complexit: Code complexity is within the maximum allowed limit.
rule_evidence.no_connector: No connector is configured for this rule. The result could not be verified through an external data source.
rule_evidence.error.RV-01: The artifact list is empty. At least one artifact is required to proceed.
rule_evidence.error.RV-02: Orphan references detected: {{count}}. The following IDs referenced in '{{source_type}}' artifacts do not exist as '{{target_type}}': {{missing_refs}}
rule_evidence.error.RV-04: Artifacts with invalid or negative numeric fields (fields: {{numeric_fields}}): {{invalid_artifacts}}
rule_evidence.error.RV-07.not_found: No marker artifact of type '{{artifact_type}}' found to indicate external registration
rule_evidence.error.RV-07.not_true: Artifact '{{artifact_id}}' of type '{{artifact_type}}' found but '{{marker_field}}' is not true
rule_evidence.error.RV-08.field_not_arra: Field '{{master_field}}' in master '{{master_id}}' is not a valid array
rule_evidence.error.RV-08.field_not_foun: Field '{{master_field}}' not found in master artifact '{{master_id}}'
rule_evidence.error.RV-08.discrepancy: Discrepancy between declared list and payload. IDs declared in '{{master_field}}' of master '{{master_id}}' not found in '{{target_type}}' artifacts: {{missing_ids}}
rule_evidence.error.RV-08.multiple_maste: Multiple artifacts of type '{{master_type}}' found ({{count}}); specify 'master_artifact_id' to disambiguate
rule_evidence.error.RV-10: No artifact of type '{{artifact_type}}' found with approved status (accepted states: {{approved_states}})
rule_evidence.ok.RV-07.found: External registration marker '{{artifact_type}}' found in artifact '{{artifact_id}}'
rule_evidence.ok.RV-10.found: Artifact '{{artifact_id}}' of type '{{artifact_type}}' found with approved status: '{{approved_status}}'
rule_evidence.no_evaluada.empty_artifact: No artifacts available to evaluate this rule.
rule_evidence.no_evaluada.RV-06: No artifacts of type '{{artifact_type}}' found to evaluate
rule_evidence.no_evaluada.RV-06.no_expec: Parameter 'expected_value' not configured — rule not applicable
rule_evidence.no_evaluada.RV-07: Parameter 'artifact_type' not configured — rule not applicable
rule_evidence.no_evaluada.RV-08: No artifact of type '{{master_type}}' found to act as the master, and 'master_artifact_id' was not specified
rule_evidence.no_evaluada.no_artifacts_o: No artifacts of type '{{artifact_type}}' in the delivery — rule not applicable
rule_evidence.error.has_open_high_priori: Artifacts with open high priority issues: {{violations}}
rule_evidence.error.meets_minimum_test_c: Artifacts not meeting minimum test coverage: {{violations}}
rule_evidence.warning.artifact_fetch_err: Could not retrieve artifact '{{ref}}' (type: {{artifact_type}}) from connector '{{connector}}': {{error}}
Could not retrieve '{{ref}}' of type {{artifact_type}} from connector '{{connector}}'. Verify that the external reference '{{ref}}' exists and is accessible with the configured credentials.
connector_field.instance_url_plane: Instance URL (leave empty for Plane Cloud)
artifact_type_help.TAREA: Tasks are work items — issues, stories or bugs — imported from a task manager such as Jira or Plane. Each artifact references a single issue by its exact key. Tasks are evaluated by rules on status, effort estimation, planning coherence, and release registration.
artifact_type_help.CODIGO: Code artifacts represent a commit, pull request or branch in a code repository. Use the exact identifier as it appears in your repo (commit hash, PR number, or branch name). Code artifacts are evaluated by traceability and quality rules.
artifact_type_help.DOCUMENTO: Documents are files or pages from your document management system — specifications, manuals or test reports. Use the document ID, path or filename as recognised by the connector. Documents are evaluated by existence and version rules.
artifact_type_help.PLAN: Plans are sprint or milestone records from a planning tool. Reference the sprint ID, milestone number or planning item key. Plan artifacts are evaluated by planning-consistency rules.
artifact_type_help.CAMBIO: Changes are RFC, release-request or change-ticket records from a change management system. Reference the change request ID or ticket number. Change artifacts are evaluated by release-registration rules.
release.no_artifacts: No deliveries associated with this release.
release.no_connectors: No active connectors found. Add one in Settings.
release.artifact_type_help_aria: Show help for this artifact type
release.browse_empty: No items found. Enter reference manually.
release.browse_error: Could not reach connector. Enter reference manually.
release.select_connector_error: Select a connector and enter the reference
